proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_olaparib` have a higher probability of `objective_response` than patients not receiving `treatment_olaparib`, after adjustment for clinical severity and other treatments.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability regression for objective_response including treatment indicators, biomarkers, disease burden, labs, comorbidities, and rare alterations; effect_estimate is the signed adjusted risk-difference coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: For treatment_olaparib, unadjusted response 0.239 vs 0.240 (n=5098 vs 44902); adjusted risk-difference coefficient 0.0041, p=0.4935.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients without `brca2_mutation`, independent of treatment and disease severity.
The response effect from `treatment_olaparib` is smaller among patients with `brca2_mutation` than among patients without `brca2_mutation`.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability regression for objective_response including treatment indicators, biomarkers, disease burden, labs, comorbidities, and rare alterations; effect_estimate is the signed adjusted risk-difference coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: For brca2_mutation, unadjusted response 0.150 vs 0.250 (n=4996 vs 45004); adjusted risk-difference coefficient -0.1020, p=1.00e-300.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability regression with treatment, marker, and treatment-by-marker interaction; effect_estimate is the adjusted risk-difference interaction coefficient for objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: The treatment_olaparib by brca2_mutation interaction showed an adjusted marginal difference-in-differences of -0.0336 with p=0.0597.
